Plaintiff Insurance Company Of The West'S Motion To Compel (Tentative Ruling Only Part 2 Of 2)
Off Calendar Oct-31-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Plaintiff Insurance Company Of The West'S Motion To Compel (Tentative Ruling Only Part 2 Of 2)

Off-calendar, duplicate entry =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Oct-31-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF INSURANCE COMPANY OF THE WEST Notice Of Motion To Compel

Plaintiff Insurance Company of the West moves for an order compelling defendant David Peralta, Jr., to Answer plaintiff's Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One; and Request for Production of Documents, Set One; and for an award of sanctions of $810 ($750 in fees and $60 in costs) incurred in bringing the motion. Because the motion is well founded and unopposed, it is GRANTED.

This case arises from an automobile accident on January 8, 2016. Plaintiff alleges that Rami Al Fakhoury, an employee of C&J Leasing, Inc. ("C&J"), for which plaintiff is the workers' compensation insurance carrier, drove a car that was hit by a vehicle owned by defendant. Plaintiff further alleges that Mr. Fakhoury was injured as a result of the collision and, because at the time he was acting in the course and scope of his employment with C&J, plaintiff has had to provide workers' compensation benefits for him. Plaintiff sues defendant for reimbursement of the cost of those benefits under Labor Code section 3852.
Plaintiff's discovery requests seek information and documents that appear relevant to plaintiff's claim against defendant. Plaintiff served the requests on defendant on September 13, 2017. Defendant has not responded to the requests. Defendant also has not opposed this motion to compel and for sanctions.

When a party fails to respond timely to interrogatories and a request for documents, all objections are waived and the Court may grant a motion to compel answers to the discovery requests and production of the requested documents. Cal. Code Civ. Proc. 2030.290(a), (b) (providing that failure to timely respond to interrogatories "waives any right to exercise the option to ... object[] to the interrogatories" and that propounding party may move to compel answers); id., 2031.300(a), (b) (providing same for document requests). In addition, sanctions shall be awarded to compensate the moving party for the expense of bringing the motion to compel, absent a showing that the party against whom sanctions are sought acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust. Id., 2030.290(c), 2031.300(c). Here, given that defendant has not opposed the motion, there is no such showing. Moreover, the sanctions sought-$750 in attorneys' fees and $60 in costs-appear to be reasonable.

Accordingly, IT IS ORDERED that within fifteen days after the date this order is entered, defendant shall answer, fully and without objections, plaintiff's Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One; and Request for Production of Documents, Set One; produce all of the requested documents in his possession, custody or control; and pay to plaintiff sanctions of $810.

Prevailing party to prepare and submit order in compliance with CRC3.1312 to Judge pro tem Jeff Wohl at jeffwohl@paulhastings.com. Judge Pro Tem:Jeff Wohl; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Oct-17-2019 Continued To Oct-31-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
